title: Living in Boulder Blog 6 - 26 - 2017: Buildings Book of Rome.

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual daily vlog update about being absorbed in digitizing a 1996 picture book about the buildings of Rome — he's editing out footnotes and references so his computer can read it aloud smoothly. He briefly covers recent bike rides with his mom, a sound-check experiment, feeling sick the past few days, and outlines his broader project pipeline: tracing the cobblestones of Rome, building a 'Legion' of 160,000 people in Boulder, and launching a Patreon, expecting most of it done by early 2018.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: worth the tedious editing work because it's a great way to learn all the buildings of Rome for his map
expects everything to come together quickly once individual pieces are done, targeting early 2018
plans to pursue it after getting surface-level website work done
prefers to do the editing work off the bike because it requires some concentration to decide what to cut
